help.regularMonitoringDesc: Check your crops regularly for early signs of disease.
help.consultExpertsDesc: Always consult with local agricultural experts for treatment decisions.
help.keepRecordsDesc: Maintain records of detected diseases and treatments applied.

agriGold.name: Agri Gold Foliar Fertilizer
agriGold.description: Agri Gold is a wonder product that prevents flower shedding, promotes more flower formation and bumper yield while enhancing healthy fruit formation and vegetative growth.
agriGold.usage: Foliar application: Mix 2-3mls of Agri Gold per 1ltrs of water (60mls/20ltrs). Drip Irrigation: Mix 5mls of Agri Gold per 1ltr of water
agriGold.benefits: Prevents flower shedding, promotes flower formation, enhances fruit formation, improves vegetative growth

omniK.name: Omni K - Potassium Nitrate Fertilizer
omniK.description: Omni K is a water-soluble fertilizer for fertigation or foliar application, supplying nitrate, nitrogen and chlorine free potassium to plants for enhanced growth and higher yields.

magnesiumNitrate.name: Magnesium Nitrate - Hexahydrate Fertilizer
magnesiumNitrate.description: Magnesium Nitrate is recommended for vegetative growth and during production stages. The high level of Magnesium contributes to the production of chlorophyll.
magnesiumNitrate.content: Water soluble Mg: 9.4% MIN, Nitrogen N: 10.5% MIN

elfertF.name: Elfert F - Trace Element Mix
elfertF.description: Elfert F is an effective foliar spray to correct trace element deficiency. Application of Trace elements is most often ignored by farmers and yet they are vital to balance the uptake of Major nutrients.
elfertF.content: Fe EDDHA 5.345%, Zn EDTA 2.672%, Mn EDTA 2.672%, Cu EDTA 0.540%, B 1.088%, MnO 0.0533%

novaPeak.name: Nova Peak 0-52-34 - Monopotassium Phosphate
novaPeak.description: Nova Peak MKP fertilizer is a high purity product that dissolves completely and quickly in water. Thanks to its very low sodium content, Nova Peak is recommended for use in any type of crop.

calciumNitrate.name: Calcium Nitrate - Nitrogen Calcium Compound
calciumNitrate.description: Calcium Nitrate helps with cell formation but it also neutralizes acids to detoxify the plant. The nitrogen component is also responsible for fuelling protein production and essentially leafy growth.
calciumNitrate.content: Nitrogen (N): 15.5%, Nitrate Nitrogen (N): 14.4%, Ammonical Nitrogen (N): 1.1%, Calcium Oxide: 26%

yaravitaCropBoost.name: Yaravita Crop Boost
yaravitaCropBoost.description: YaraVita CROP BOOST is a concentrated phosphorous foliar fertilizer that provides balanced nutrients that help your crop to unlock its hidden yield potential.

novaMap.name: Nova Map 12-61-0 - Soluble Fertilizer
novaMap.description: Nova MAP 12-61-0, Monoammonium Phosphate is ideal for use in the initial growth phase of all crops, immediately before and after seeding and planting/transplanting.
novaMap.usage: Recommended dilution rate, for stock solutions = 15 kg / 100liter water

nutriplantOrganic.name: Nutriplant Organic Plus Fertilizer
nutriplantOrganic.description: Nutriplant organicplus fertilizer (NOPF) is composed of both micro and macro nutrients containing multiple small molecules of amino acids, peptides, chitosan chelate.
nutriplantOrganic.usage: Seasonal Crops: Mix 5-10 mls of NOPF in 20 litres of Water, Perennial Crops: Mix 10-20 mls of NOPF in 20 litres of water

yaraVeraAmidas.name: Yara Vera Amidas
yaraVeraAmidas.description: It is a granular, white coloured, highly soluble fertilizer and contains the highest nitrogen among all nitrogenous fertilizers.
yaraVeraAmidas.content: Chemical formula is CO(NH2)2 and contains 46% N (nitrogen)

maizeRust.symptoms: Reddish-brown pustules on leaves, stems, and husks. Leaves may turn yellow and die prematurely.
maizeRust.treatment: Apply fungicides containing azoxystrobin or pyraclostrobin. Remove infected plant debris. Plant resistant varieties like DK777 or SC719.
maizeRust.prevention: Ensure proper spacing (75cm between rows), avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use certified seeds.
maizeRust.severity: High - Can reduce yield by 30-50% if not controlled early

coffeeLeafRust.symptoms: Yellow-orange spots on leaves that turn brown, premature leaf drop, reduced photosynthesis.
coffeeLeafRust.treatment: Apply copper-based fungicides or systemic fungicides. Prune infected branches. Maintain good air circulation.
coffeeLeafRust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties like Catimor, avoid dense planting, monitor regularly, and maintain proper shade.
coffeeLeafRust.severity: Critical - Can destroy entire plantations if not managed

tomatoBlight.symptoms: Dark brown lesions on leaves, stems, and fruits. White fungal growth in humid conditions.
tomatoBlight.treatment: Remove infected plants immediately. Apply copper fungicides or chlorothalonil. Improve air circulation.
tomatoBlight.prevention: Water at soil level, avoid overhead irrigation, use resistant varieties, and maintain proper spacing.
tomatoBlight.severity: High - Can spread rapidly in wet conditions

bananaWilt.symptoms: Yellowing leaves, wilting, internal vascular discoloration, plant death.
bananaWilt.treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants. Apply systemic fungicides. Use clean planting material.
bananaWilt.prevention: Use disease-free suckers, avoid waterlogging, maintain good drainage, and rotate crops.
bananaWilt.severity: Critical - Can wipe out entire plantations

cassavaMosaic.symptoms: Yellow mosaic patterns on leaves, stunted growth, reduced tuber yield.
cassavaMosaic.treatment: Remove infected plants. Use virus-free cuttings. Control whitefly vectors.
cassavaMosaic.prevention: Use certified disease-free cuttings, control whiteflies, and practice crop rotation.
cassavaMosaic.severity: Medium - Reduces yield but plants may survive

maize.planting: Plant 2-3 seeds per hole, 5cm deep, 75cm between rows, 25cm within rows.
maize.season: Plant at onset of rains (March-April in Uganda).
maize.fertilizer: Apply NPK 17:17:17 at planting, top-dress with CAN at 6 weeks.
maize.harvesting: Harvest when kernels are hard and moisture is 20-25%.

coffee.season: Plant at onset of long rains (March-May).
coffee.fertilizer: Apply NPK 20:10:10 annually, organic matter every 2 years.
coffee.harvesting: Harvest red ripe cherries, process within 24 hours.

beans.planting: Plant 2-3 seeds per hole, 3cm deep, 50cm between rows.
beans.season: Plant in both short and long rains.
beans.fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, top-dress with CAN.
beans.harvesting: Harvest when pods are dry and seeds rattle.

general.fertilizer: Use organic fertilizers like compost, manure, or NPK fertilizers based on soil test results. Apply during planting and growing seasons. Organic options improve soil health long-term.
general.irrigation: Water deeply but less frequently. Early morning watering reduces evaporation. Use drip irrigation for efficiency. Monitor soil moisture regularly.
general.pestControl: Use integrated pest management: natural predators, crop rotation, resistant varieties, and minimal chemical use. Start with cultural practices.
general.soilHealth: Test soil pH annually, add organic matter, practice crop rotation, and avoid over-tilling. Healthy soil = healthy crops.
general.weather: Monitor weather forecasts regularly. Adjust planting times based on rainfall patterns. Protect crops during extreme weather events.
general.market: Research market prices before planting. Consider contract farming. Diversify crops to spread risk. Store properly to sell during off-season.

• Prepare soil
• Plant at proper depth
• Water after planting

Watering:

• Water early morning
• Check soil moisture
• Use drip irrigation

• Harvest at peak ripeness

• Handle gently
• Store properly

• Use organic when possible
• Apply at right stages
• Don't over-fertilize

• Protect from extreme weather
